title: Sith battlelord
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}}
{{Youmay|the Sith class|[[Sith Battlelords (article)|''Sith Battlelords'']], the [[Wizards.com]] article}}
{{Otheruses|Battlelord}}

{{Quote|These warriors, the Sith Battlelords, were fearsome and hardy, having demonstrated a willingness to die for the Sith in an attempt to curry favor and enhance their own power.|The Jedi publication ''Speculations on Tactics of the Sith''|<ref name="AMA" />}}
The '''Sith battlelords''', also known as '''Battlelords''', were a class of [[Soldier/Legends|warrior]] who served the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Rivan]] during the [[New Sith Wars]]. [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] battlelords were leaders within Rivan's [[Army/Legends|armies]] and, through a series of complex rituals involving the application of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]], were bonded to the [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] they commanded via [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. The use of Sith battlelords reduced desertion rates within the Sith armies and, for a [[Time/Legends|time]], seemed likely to turn the tide of the New Sith Wars in the favor of the Sith. However, Rivan's battlelord forces were annihilated by a [[Force storm (wormhole)|Force storm]] generated by an artifact known as the [[Darkstaff]], and the Sith ultimately abandoned the practice of creating battlelords.

Over time, the precise details of how to create Sith battlelords was lost, and, during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]] [[Phylus Mon]] and the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Karae Nalvas]] sought to rediscover the knowledge of battlelord creation. The pair traveled to [[Almas Sith fortress|Rivan's fortress]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Almas]] and discovered a secret chamber within the facility, where they found a tome that detailed the stages of the battlelord rituals. However, their attempts at carrying out the rituals were unsuccessful.

==Organization and creation==
{{Quote|There's a long, drawn-out ritual. All sorts of Sith "magic" is involved, and I don't want to know exactly what happens. But at the end, the person who's supposed to become a battlelord is laid down and cut open. Then he bleeds, and while he's bleeding, his troops pass by and track through his blood.|A slicer, on the rituals to create Sith battlelords|<ref name="SB">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/lf20050901sithbattlelords|text=Sith Battlelords}}</ref>}}
[[Sith/Legends|Sith]] battlelords were a class of [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]] who served within the [[Army/Legends|armies]] of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Rivan]]<ref name="AMA" /> during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[New Sith Wars]],<ref name="EOTJ" /> a millennium-long conflict that began in [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]] and was fought between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[New Sith|resurgent Sith]].<ref name="Jedi vs Sith">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> Chosen from the ranks of Rivan's forces, battlelords served as the leaders of Sith [[Regiment/Legends|regiments]] that were deployed against [[Jedi/Legends|members]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="AMA" />

Individuals chosen to become Sith battlelords would undergo a series of complex rituals that bonded them to their [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] via [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="AMA" /> The rituals took place at sites strong in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]<ref name="SB" /> and, according to most reports, would take place within an altar room inside a temple. The rituals drew upon the dark side and used [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]], inflicting pain upon the prospective battlelord and bringing the being to the verge of [[Death/Legends|death]]. Some did not survive the ceremony. According to the Jedi publication ''[[Speculations on Tactics of the Sith]]'', some people claimed that aspiring battlelords were drained of all their [[Blood/Legends|blood]], although the publication also noted that there was no exact consensus on how individuals were brought to a near-death condition.<ref name="AMA" />

Following performance of the rituals, some form of interaction between the [[Sleep/Legends|unconscious]] leader and their followers was required to complete the leader's transformation into a Sith battlelord. Followers normally paraded past the lifeless body of their leader<ref name="SB" /> for about an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]],<ref name="AMA" /> walking through the leader's blood. The leader's condition had to then stabilize without an application of the Force or medical intervention, and, if the leader survived,<ref name="SB" /> the being was returned to full health. With the process complete, a [[Force-bond/Legends|Force bond]] was forged between the subordinates and their leader, which signified the leader's successful transformation into a Sith battlelord.<ref name="AMA" />

==History==
{{Quote|The advent of the battlelords threatened for a time to further the tide of the Sith wars in the favor of the dark side.|The Jedi publication ''Speculations on Tactics of the Sith''|<ref name="AMA" />}}
[[File:Darthriv.jpg|200px|thumb|Darth Rivan, a Sith Lord who pioneered the creation of Sith battlelords]]
The creation of Sith battlelords was pioneered by Darth Rivan, a [[Zelosian]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] who ruled the Sith during the New Sith Wars from a [[Almas Sith fortress|fortress]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Almas]].<ref name="EOTJ" /> The battlelords were developed in response to high desertion rates among the Sith armies, which had reduced the coordination of the Sith military forces and posed a threat to the success of their war effort,<ref name="AMA" /> and Rivan eventually perfected the process of Sith battlelord creation.<ref name="SB" /> Rivan recorded the details of the battlelord ritual inside a secret chamber in his fortress on Almas, and information on the nature of the bond between the warrior leaders and their subordinates was also detailed in inscriptions on the walls of various [[Sith temple/Legends|Sith temples]].<ref name="AMA" />

The Sith were initially startled by the effects of Sith battlelords on the battlefield, because the bonded leaders brought a much greater degree of cohesion to the Sith armies than had been expected. A [[Unidentified Sith warrior|Sith warrior]] bonded to the Sith battlelord [[Farh j'Hien]] deserted the Sith forces and was captured by the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kei Loo Bross]]. The Jedi observed how the warrior was in pain due to his separation from j'Hien, as a result of the Force bond between j'Hien and the warrior. In a transcript, Bross noted how the battlelords proved to be an effective tool for the Sith. For a time, their usage threatened to turn the tide of the New Sith Wars in the favor of the Sith. However, Rivan was betrayed by [[Darsin|his apprentice]] before he could build a full army of bonded leaders,<ref name="SB" /> and when Rivan gained possession of the [[Darkstaff]], an ancient Sith artifact that fed on [[Force essence]]s of individuals,<ref name="EOTJ" /> it created a [[Force storm (wormhole)|Force storm]] that wiped out his entire battlelord force and [[Time travel/Legends|transported]] him into the future.<ref name="EOTJ" />

The Jedi soon realized that killing a battlelord released the warriors bonded to the leader, who would often then flee, so the Jedi began specifically targeting Sith battlelords. As a result, Sith battlelords no longer met their required purpose, and the Sith discontinued their usage. Over time, the precise details on how to create the warrior leaders were lost and only vague references to the battlelords came to remain in historical records. At some point, a section devoted to the subject of Sith battlelords was included in the Jedi publication ''Speculations on Tactics of the Sith''.<ref name="AMA" />

Over a thousand years later, during the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Len Markus]] gave the Darkstaff to the [[Chevin/Legends|Chevin]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]] [[Phylus Mon]] so that Mon could repair the artifact, which had became damaged when Markus [[Cularin's disappearance|seized]] it from the [[Cularin asteroid belt]]. To carry out his task, Mon traveled to the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]], where Almas was located, and planned to recover knowledge on the creation of Sith battlelords from Rivan's fortress. Since the Darkstaff desired the Force essences of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings, Mon believed that the performance of the battlelord rituals would repair the artifact, because the Force essences of the involved individuals were manipulated by the creation rituals. In addition, Mon hoped to be able to use the techniques to bond armies of slaves to himself or others.<ref name="AMA" />

Mon allied himself with the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Karae Nalvas]], who also wanted to learn the secrets of Sith battlelord creation. However, the Chevin secretly planned to murder Nalvas once the Dark Jedi had became a bonded leader, to find out what would happen when such a being was killed. Mon and Nalvas [[Raid on the Almas Sith fortress|entered]] the Almas Sith fortress and accessed the facility's battlelord chamber, where they discovered a tome that described how to carry out the battlelord rituals. Using their newly discovered knowledge, the pair attempted to carry out the battlelord rituals, planning to bind the slaves [[Gorak]], [[Kaarror]], [[Nalz]], and [[Yerj]], and the captured Jedi [[Constanten (Tarasin)|Constanten]] and [[Valiri]], to Mon. However, the ceremony was unsuccessful due to the presence of the Darkstaff, and instead resulted in the Force essences of Nalvas, the slaves, and the two Jedi being ripped from their bodies.<ref name="AMA" />

At some point after that incident, a [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] posted a log on the subject of Sith battlelords on one of the planet [[Cularin/Legends|Cularin's]] [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] nodes. In the log, the slicer detailed how battlelords were created, and speculated that Rivan might have specifically constructed the Almas Sith fortress to use as a facility for testing and refining the creation of battlelords.<ref name="SB" /> Len Markus regained possession of the Darkstaff following Mon's repair attempt,<ref name="TE">{{LivingForce|EndTimes: The Eye}}</ref> and, by [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref>''The Eye'' is set a short time after the Living Force adventure ''[[Heart of Evil: A Hero Seeks Not Vengeance|A Hero Seeks Not Vengeance]]'', which is set concurrently with the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]], an event that is dated to 19 BBY by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the Darkstaff had imbued Markus with some of the powers of a Sith battlelord, creating a Force bond between Markus and his [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] bodyguard [[Kluuus]].<ref name="TE" />

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|I guess Rivan designed these battlelords. The basic idea was to eliminate insubordination as an option for soldiers and make it difficult for someone who's in charge of soldiers to mistreat them.|A slicer, on the subject of Sith battlelords|<ref name="SB" />}}
Sith battlelords were tough, imposing individuals who sought to increase their own personal power by attempting to gain favor with their Sith leaders, and were willing to die in the process. They also possessed a maniacal blood-rage that drove them to push their troops to their limits.<ref name="AMA" /> Battlelords gained thickened skin as a result of their bonding rituals, increasing their defensive capabilities.<ref name="SB" /> The majority of the warrior leaders were [[Gender/Legends|males]].<ref name="AMA" />

The battlelord rituals created a near-physical bond between the warrior leaders and their troops.<ref name="AMA" /> Depending upon the strength of the ritual used to connect them together, bonded soldiers could go between five and fifteen [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] from their battlelord<ref name="SB" /> and were compelled to do anything that their battlelord commanded, or else be left behind.<ref name="AMA" /> Any troops who strayed too far from their Sith battlelord would experience intense abdominal pain and a throbbing headache for twelve hours, eventually dying unless they returned to close proximity of their bonded leader.<ref name="SB" /> Unless certain magics were undone, separation of any member from the rest of the regiment also led to intense physical pain being felt by all other members, and caused the warrior leader to have visions of his or her own destruction. As a result, bonded Sith armies dealt with deserters harshly, and captured deserters were presented to the battlelords for punishment.<ref name="AMA" />

If bonded soldiers tried to assault the Sith battlelord to which they were linked, the attackers would become beset with pain and would eventually die. Furthermore, if warriors succeeded in drawing their battlelord master's blood, the battlelord would be unaffected by the damage and one of the bonded troopers would be hurt instead. In addition, if a Sith battlelord fell under attack from an opponent to which it was not bonded, the battlelord could sometimes transfer any physical wounds it received to any bonded soldiers that were located within thirty [[Meter/Legends|meters]] of the battlelord. A battlelord could also enhance its own attacks by summoning power from bonded soldiers within a thirty meter proximity, which had the effect of causing the bonded soldiers to become dazed.<ref name="SB" /> The death of a Sith battlelord released the warriors that were bonded to the lord.<ref name="AMA" /> The bond between a battlelord and its followers could also be severed by an application of the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] power [[Force Light]].<ref name="SB" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Sith battlelords were created by [[Morrie Mullins (real person)|Morrie Mullins]] and were first mentioned in ''[[A Mon Alone]]'', a [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|role-playing]] adventure that formed part of the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|Living Force]] campaign.<ref name="AMA" /> They later featured in the [[Wizards.com]] article [[Sith Battlelords (article)|''Sith Battlelords'']] which was also written by Mullins,<ref name="SB" /> and were mentioned in the role-playing scenarios [[EndTimes: The Eye|''The Eye'']]—written by Mullins and [[Jae Walker]]—<ref name="TE" />and ''[[Echoes of the Jedi]]'', an adventure in the [[Dawn of Defiance]] role-playing campaign that was written by [[Jean-François Boivin]] and [[Abel G. Peña]].<ref name="EOTJ" />

''A Mon Alone'', ''Sith Battlelords'', and ''Echoes of the Jedi'' all give differing reasons for why the Sith discontinued the use of Sith battlelords.<ref name="AMA" /><ref name="SB" /><ref name="EOTJ" /> ''A Mon Alone'' claims that it was because the Jedi began specifically targeting battlelords when in battle against the Sith,<ref name="AMA" /> while ''Sith Battlelords'' claims that Rivan was betrayed by his apprentice before he could create an army of battlelords,<ref name="SB" /> and ''Echoes of the Jedi'' claims that Rivan's battlelord army was destroyed by a Force storm.<ref name="EOTJ" /> This article tries to integrate the three above explanations. There is also disagreement among the sources regarding the appropriate capitalization of the Sith battlelords' name.<ref name="AMA" /> ''A Mon Alone'' refers to them as "Sith Battlelords,"<ref name="AMA" /> while the ''Sith Battlelords'' article and ''The Eye'' refer to them as "Sith battlelords"<ref name="SB" /><ref name="TE" /> and ''Echoes of the Jedi'' merely calls them "Battlelords".<ref name="EOTJ" /> This article uses the capitalization from ''Sith Battlelords'' and ''The Eye'', as they were published after ''A Mon Alone''.<ref name="SB" /><ref name="TE" />
